methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals institutional concentration of AI education infrastructure in the state capital, creating a metropolitan capability hub that may struggle to radiate skills beyond the Richmond metro boundary without intentional distribution mechanisms.

signals.title: RC-RV partners with ed2go to launch innovative, on-demand workforce training programs - Roanoke College
signals.signal_reading: Roanoke College's adoption of ed2go's platform-based workforce training represents shallow-but-distributed AI exposure—accessible on-demand tools that democratize access but may not build deep local capability or peer learning networks in rural regions.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia signals potential for distributed, locally-owned capability building that could create peer networks and deep skills outside traditional tech hubs if sustained beyond grant funding.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: NoVA school systems wrestling with AI classroom integration reflects the concentration of early-adopter institutional capacity in wealthy suburbs, where resource advantages allow experimentation that rural districts cannot yet afford or staff.

signals.title: Pittsylvania County has 2,050 jobs at risk in data center tax debate. Will a change in law cause them to disappear?
signals.signal_reading: Pittsylvania County's 2,050 at-risk data center jobs reveal the fragility of infrastructure-dependent economic development that delivers neither deep AI skills nor distributed capability—just precarious employment vulnerable to policy shifts and automation.
